name: Fortaleza (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the diagonal cross — the same blue as the celestial sphere on the Brazilian national flag

description: A white field with a blue saltire (diagonal cross) running from corner to corner; the municipal coat of arms is placed at the intersection of the diagonals at the centre of the flag.
history: The municipal symbols of Fortaleza were prescribed by Municipal Law No. 1,316 of 11 November 1958, on a bill by city councillor Agamemnon Frota Leitão. The flag was designed by the abolitionist Isaac Correia do Amaral. The blue of the saltire is specified to match the blue of the celestial sphere on the Brazilian national flag, identifying the city with the nation. The coat of arms — itself a 19th-century design attributed to the politician and writer Tristão de Alencar Araripe — depicts elements of the city's foundation as the colonial Forte de Nossa Senhora da Assunção.

features.symbolism: a blue saltire on a white field — the saltire's blue matches the celestial-sphere blue of the Brazilian national flag, identifying Fortaleza with the wider Brazilian nation

features.role: coat of arms of the Municipality of Fortaleza
features.symbolism: the city's coat of arms is placed at the intersection of the diagonals; designed in the 19th century by Tristão de Alencar Araripe, it commemorates the foundation of the city around the colonial Forte de Nossa Senhora da Assunção and its role as the capital of Ceará
